introduction: An uncommon disease characterized by periodontal destruction, often in the absence of overt gingival inflammation.

features: Affects the first permanent molar and incisors
Actinobacillus, Actinomycetes comitans has been isolated from the affected sites
Results in drifting and loss of the first permanent molar and incisors

investigations: Radiology may reveal marked bone loss interdentally, inter-radicularly and apically

non_drug: Control of plaque bacteria by use of antiseptic solution
Establishing a healthy gingival and periodontal attachment
Bone regenerative techniques e.g. using polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) membranes, Bio-Oss, Bio-membrane
drug: Metronidazole - Adult: 200 mg orally every 8 hours for 5 days
Metronidazole - Child 1 - 3 years: 50 mg orally every 8 hours
Metronidazole - Child 3 - 7 years: 100 mg every 12 hours
Metronidazole - Child 7 - 10 years: 100 mg every 8 hours
Metronidazole - Child 10 - 18 years: 200 mg every 8 hours
Tetracycline - Adult: 250 mg orally daily for up to 21 days
Child under 12 years: metronidazole and amoxicillin (or erythromycin for those sensitive to penicillin)
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Tetracyclines should not be given to children under 12 years
